req.title: Interest accrual
req.statement: A savings account shall accrue interest daily using the tier rate and credit interest monthly on the last business day.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-016 mandates daily interest accrual using tier rates and monthly crediting on the last business day via /cron/interest. Test design covers: (1) EP positive – a standard mid-tier account receives correct interest credit and ledger entry on a valid last-business-day trigger; (2) NEG negative – triggering on a non-last-business-day must not credit interest and must report a reconciliation variance; (3) BVA boundary – an account balance sitting exactly on a tier boundary (e.g. $10,000.00) must apply the correct tier rate, not the adjacent one; (4) STC state transition – account progresses from 'accruing' to 'credited' state after month-end job, verifying the full state machine; (5) DT decision table – zero-balance account must accrue zero interest and produce no ledger entry, validating the degenerate input column.

tests.name: test_interest_credited_on_last_business_day
preconditions: Savings account exists with balance in mid tier (e.g. $5,000)
Daily accrual records have been written for all days in the month
Trigger date is the last business day of the month
expected: HTTP 200 from /cron/interest
Response body contains credited_accounts list including <account_id>
Transaction of type INTEREST_CREDIT appears for <account_id>
Ledger entry debit INTEREST_EXPENSE and credit SAVINGS_ACCOUNT balance posted
Credited amount equals sum of daily accruals for the month
trace: R-FIN-016: interest credited monthly on last business day
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from decimal import Decimal
from <helper_fn> import get_last_business_day, compute_expected_interest
from <fixture> import savings_account_mid_tier, api_base_url, auth_headers

def test_interest_credited_on_last_business_day(
    savings_account_mid_tier, api_base_url, auth_headers
):
    account_id = savings_account_mid_tier["account_id"]
    run_date = get_last_business_day()
    expected_interest = compute_expected_interest(account_id, run_date)

    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/cron/interest",
        json={"run_date": str(run_date)},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    body = resp.json()
    assert account_id in body.get("credited_accounts", [])

    txn_resp = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/accounts/{account_id}/transactions",
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    txns = txn_resp.json()["transactions"]
    interest_txns = [t for t in txns if t["type"] == "INTEREST_CREDIT"]
    assert len(interest_txns) >= 1
    credited = Decimal(str(interest_txns[-1]["amount"]))
    assert credited == expected_interest

    ledger_resp = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/accounts/{account_id}/ledger",
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    entries = ledger_resp.json()["entries"]
    assert any(e["type"] == "INTEREST_EXPENSE" for e in entries)

tests.name: test_interest_not_credited_on_non_last_business_day
preconditions: Savings account exists with positive balance
Trigger date is NOT the last business day of the month (e.g. mid-month)
actions: GET /accounts/<account_id>/transactions to confirm no new INTEREST_CREDIT
expected: HTTP 422 or 200 with empty credited_accounts list
No INTEREST_CREDIT transaction posted for <account_id>
Reconciliation report flags a variance or no-op for the run date
trace: R-FIN-016: credit interest monthly on the last business day
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from <helper_fn> import get_mid_month_date, get_transaction_count
from <fixture> import savings_account_mid_tier, api_base_url, auth_headers

def test_interest_not_credited_on_non_last_business_day(
    savings_account_mid_tier, api_base_url, auth_headers
):
    account_id = savings_account_mid_tier["account_id"]
    run_date = get_mid_month_date()
    txn_count_before = get_transaction_count(account_id, "INTEREST_CREDIT", auth_headers)

    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/cron/interest",
        json={"run_date": str(run_date)},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 422)
    body = resp.json()
    assert account_id not in body.get("credited_accounts", [])

    txn_count_after = get_transaction_count(account_id, "INTEREST_CREDIT", auth_headers)
    assert txn_count_after == txn_count_before

    recon_resp = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/cron/interest/reconciliation",
        params={"run_date": str(run_date)},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert recon_resp.status_code == 200
    report = recon_resp.json()
    assert report.get("variance_detected") is True or report.get("skipped_reason") is not None

tests.name: test_interest_applies_correct_tier_at_exact_tier_boundary
preconditions: Savings account exists with balance exactly at tier boundary (e.g. $10,000.00)
Tier table: tier1 applies for balance < $10,000; tier2 applies for balance >= $10,000
Daily accrual records have been written for all days in the month
Trigger date is the last business day of the month
expected: HTTP 200 from /cron/interest
Account is in credited_accounts
Credited interest is calculated using tier2 rate (not tier1)
Amount matches tier2_rate * balance * days / 365 within rounding tolerance
trace: R-FIN-016: accrue interest daily using the tier rate
R-FIN-016: boundary between tier1 and tier2 at $10,000.00
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from decimal import Decimal, ROUND_HALF_UP
from <helper_fn> import get_last_business_day, days_in_month
from <fixture> import savings_account_at_tier_boundary, api_base_url, auth_headers, tier_rates

def test_interest_applies_correct_tier_at_exact_tier_boundary(
    savings_account_at_tier_boundary, api_base_url, auth_headers, tier_rates
):
    account_id = savings_account_at_tier_boundary["account_id"]
    balance = Decimal("10000.00")
    run_date = get_last_business_day()
    tier2_rate = Decimal(str(tier_rates["tier2"]))
    days = days_in_month(run_date)
    expected = (balance * tier2_rate * days / 365).quantize(Decimal("0.01"), ROUND_HALF_UP)

    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/cron/interest",
        json={"run_date": str(run_date)},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert account_id in resp.json().get("credited_accounts", [])

    txn_resp = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/accounts/{account_id}/transactions",
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    txns = txn_resp.json()["transactions"]
    credit = next(t for t in reversed(txns) if t["type"] == "INTEREST_CREDIT")
    actual = Decimal(str(credit["amount"]))
    assert actual == expected, f"Expected tier2 interest {expected}, got {actual}"

tests.name: test_account_state_transitions_from_accruing_to_credited
preconditions: Savings account is in ACCRUING state (daily accruals posted, month not yet closed)
Trigger date is the last business day of the month
actions: GET /accounts/<account_id> to confirm state is ACCRUING before job
GET /accounts/<account_id> to confirm state transitions to CREDITED
expected: Pre-job account status equals ACCRUING
HTTP 200 from /cron/interest
Post-job account status equals CREDITED
State transition audit log records ACCRUING -> CREDITED
trace: R-FIN-016: system job triggers accrual; month-end job triggers crediting
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from <helper_fn> import get_last_business_day
from <fixture> import savings_account_accruing, api_base_url, auth_headers

def test_account_state_transitions_from_accruing_to_credited(
    savings_account_accruing, api_base_url, auth_headers
):
    account_id = savings_account_accruing["account_id"]
    run_date = get_last_business_day()

    pre = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/accounts/{account_id}", headers=auth_headers
    ).json()
    assert pre["interest_state"] == "ACCRUING"

    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/cron/interest",
        json={"run_date": str(run_date)},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200

    post = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/accounts/{account_id}", headers=auth_headers
    ).json()
    assert post["interest_state"] == "CREDITED"

    audit_resp = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/accounts/{account_id}/audit", headers=auth_headers
    )
    audit = audit_resp.json()["events"]
    transition = next(
        (e for e in audit if e.get("from") == "ACCRUING" and e.get("to") == "CREDITED"),
        None,
    )
    assert transition is not None

tests.name: test_zero_balance_account_accrues_no_interest_and_no_ledger_entry
preconditions: Savings account exists with balance of $0.00
Trigger date is the last business day of the month
actions: GET /accounts/<account_id>/ledger to verify no new ledger entry
expected: HTTP 200 from /cron/interest
Account is NOT in credited_accounts (zero interest scenario)
No INTEREST_CREDIT transaction exists for the account this month
No ledger debit/credit entry posted for zero-balance account
trace: R-FIN-016: accrue interest daily using tier rate – $0 balance produces $0 accrual
R-FIN-016: ledger entry per account – no entry when interest amount is zero
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests
from <helper_fn> import get_last_business_day, get_current_month_range
from <fixture> import savings_account_zero_balance, api_base_url, auth_headers

def test_zero_balance_account_accrues_no_interest_and_no_ledger_entry(
    savings_account_zero_balance, api_base_url, auth_headers
):
    account_id = savings_account_zero_balance["account_id"]
    run_date = get_last_business_day()
    month_start, month_end = get_current_month_range(run_date)

    resp = requests.post(
        f"{api_base_url}/cron/interest",
        json={"run_date": str(run_date)},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert account_id not in resp.json().get("credited_accounts", [])

    txn_resp = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/accounts/{account_id}/transactions",
        params={"from": str(month_start), "to": str(month_end), "type": "INTEREST_CREDIT"},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    assert txn_resp.status_code == 200
    assert txn_resp.json()["transactions"] == []

    ledger_resp = requests.get(
        f"{api_base_url}/accounts/{account_id}/ledger",
        params={"from": str(month_start), "to": str(month_end)},
        headers=auth_headers,
    )
    entries = ledger_resp.json()["entries"]
    interest_entries = [e for e in entries if e.get("type") == "INTEREST_EXPENSE"]
    assert interest_entries == []
